contact.opening_note: Term time only. Please leave a message if we're busy with the children.

staff.name: Maria Reynolds
staff.bio: Maria leads with warmth and vision, bringing extensive experience in early years education, safeguarding, and curriculum planning. She is the first point of contact for new families.

staff.name: Jemma Pugh
staff.bio: Jemma brings enthusiasm, reliability, and a deeply caring approach to her role. She excels in supporting children's individual needs and planning engaging, play-based activities.

news.title: Summer Term is in full swing!
news.body: The children are loving outdoor learning this term — from bug hunts to water play, there's never a dull moment in Ape Dale!

news.title: Breakfast with the Easter Bunny — what a morning!
news.body: Thank you to everyone who came along to our Easter fundraiser. We raised brilliant funds and had so much fun together.

news.title: We're rated Good by Ofsted!
news.body: We're delighted to share our latest Ofsted inspection result — Good across all areas. A huge well done to our wonderful team!

events.name: Breakfast with the Easter Bunny
events.description: A firm annual favourite — watch our Facebook page for the confirmed date!

events.name: Coffee Morning
events.description: Cake, chat, and community. All families welcome!

events.name: Quiz Night
events.description: Test your knowledge — all proceeds go directly to the pre-school.

admissions.intro: We accept applications year-round, with places allocated on a first-come, first-served basis. We'd love you to come and visit first.
admissions.funding_2yr: Some 2-year-olds may also qualify — please ask us

faq.a: Children can join from age 2, starting at the beginning of the next term after they become eligible.

All 3 & 4-year-olds receive 15 free hours per week. Working parents who each earn at least the National Minimum Wage for 16 hours a week (and under £100,000/year) may qualify for 30 hours. Some 2-year-olds also qualify.
We run Monday to Wednesday, 9am–3pm. Sessions can be morning, afternoon, or full-day. We recommend a minimum of 4 sessions per week to help children settle well.

faq.q: Do you support children with SEN?
faq.a: Absolutely. We work closely with families and follow the SEN Code of Practice to ensure every child gets the support they need.

faq.q: How long will my child attend?
faq.a: Until they transition to Reception class in the September after their 4th birthday. We work hard to make this transition as smooth and positive as possible.
